content_markdown:
In the two circuits X and Y below, each cell has an e.m.f. $E$ and negligible internal resistance. Each resistor has a resistance $R$.

The power dissipated in circuit X is $P$.

The best estimate for the power dissipated in circuit Y is

diagram_metadata.description: Two circuit diagrams. Circuit X shows a single cell with e.m.f. E connected to a single resistor R. Circuit Y shows a single cell with e.m.f. E connected to two resistors, each of resistance R, connected in parallel.

vibe_explanation: In circuit X, the power is $P = \frac{E^2}{R}$. In circuit Y, the two resistors are in parallel, so the equivalent resistance is $R_{eq} = \frac{R}{2}$. The power dissipated in circuit Y is $P_Y = \frac{E^2}{R_{eq}} = \frac{E^2}{R/2} = 2\frac{E^2}{R} = 2P$.
